message.content:
Adversaries may employ internal proxies to manage command and control (C2) traffic within a compromised environment, leveraging tools like HTRAN, ZXProxy, and ZXPortMap. This technique helps reduce the number of outbound connections, maintain resiliency, and blend C2 traffic with legitimate network activity. Commonly, adversaries use peer-to-peer (P2P) networking protocols such as SMB to facilitate these connections. Notable groups like APT29, APT39, and Lazarus Group have utilized internal proxies to obscure C2 destinations and maintain communication channels even when direct internet access is unavailable. The impact includes enhanced stealth and persistence within the network, complicating detection and response efforts.

Mitigation strategies include network segmentation to limit lateral movement, monitoring for unusual internal traffic patterns, and employing endpoint detection and response (EDR) tools to identify and block proxy tools. Regularly updating and patching systems can also reduce the risk of exploitation by known proxy tools.
